journal_entry.rationale: Inventory is an asset account that increases when purchased. Cash (also an asset) decreases when paid out. Debit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) indicates one asset increase - correctly mapped to Inventory. Credit tuple (0,0,0,1,0,0) indicates one asset decrease - correctly mapped to Cash. No tax information provided, so no tax lines added. This is a straightforward asset-for-asset exchange.

output.reason: Purchasing inventory increases the Inventory asset account. This is 1 asset increase on the debit side.

debit_classifier.reason: Purchasing inventory increases the Inventory asset account. This is 1 asset increase on the debit side.

output.reason: Cash is leaving the business to purchase inventory. This is an asset decrease (cash account credited). Inventory is an asset acquired on the debit side, not classified here.

credit_classifier.reason: Cash is leaving the business to purchase inventory. This is an asset decrease (cash account credited). Inventory is an asset acquired on the debit side, not classified here.
